summary: A proof beat is strongest when it demonstrates one claim in a concrete situation instead of asserting superiority.
principles: Show the claim operating in a specific use case.
Prefer visible behavior, measured result, or side-by-side demonstration over abstract adjectives.
Keep the proof proportional to the evidence the user or research supplied.
apply_when: The product has performance, quality, speed, camera, workflow, or sensory claims.
The audience is skeptical or expert enough to distrust generic hype.
The ad needs to justify a premium price or technical advantage.
avoid_when: No evidence exists for the claim.
The proof would require a fake benchmark or unsupported comparison.
The scene cannot visually demonstrate the claim without hallucination risk.
